title: agropyron repens subsp. majus
intro: Agropyron repens subsp. majus, also known as creeping couch or common couch, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Agropyron repens subsp. majus include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and treating wounds due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. Agropyron repens subsp. majus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
